what_it_does: Lists pass/fail completion criteria for the Concurrent Cognition build, grouped by subsystem (G0–G9), each with FUNCTION and FORM verification bars, current status, and references to review folds.
observations: Verification requires both FUNCTION (demonstrated by real use) and FORM (design-critic pass) bars to go green.

Criteria are grouped into G0 spike, G1 substrate, G2 role registry, G3 rule engine, G4 staged queue, G5 activation contexts, G6 voice coupling, G7 live view, G8 model/capability registry, and G9 governance.
Many criteria are marked ✅ by use, several are ◑ needs-tim, and one product-face item is ☐ design-rubric.
The document repeatedly binds itself to R1-FOLD corrections: real resource ceiling, parallelism in the cognition driver, run:// addressing, net-new injection, and enforced rule determinism.
It states governing laws/invariants: registry-driven, rule-based routing, deterministic gates, fail loud, schema-additive, claude -p floor, reflects-never-owns.
The concurrency cap figure is visibly corrected inline multiple times (32 [corrected; see first note]), showing editorial churn.
G7 includes a binding expansion reframing the cognition view as an operable craft surface for authoring roles, rules, and modes.
Acceptance tests are cited by name with pass counts (e.g., chat_parts_acceptance 65✓, voice_parts_acceptance 24/24).
standouts: C0.5 treats the measured resource ceiling as the gate that matters most before any fan-out.
C3.1 bans now()/random/order/partials from rules and enforces determinism adversarially.
C9.2 encodes the claude -p floor as an unforgeable invariant with a standing regression test.
G7's expansion makes the canvas both a live dynamic visualization and an authoring surface for registry data.
conventions: References external review artifacts (R1-FOLD, R2-FOLD) as binding correction layers.
Uses run:// as the canonical address scheme and explicitly rejects swarm://.
Labels criteria with commit hashes, measured dates, and acceptance-test pass counts.
Capitalizes group titles and numbers criteria (G0, C1.1, etc.).
concerns: The concurrency cap is corrected inline repeatedly, which may confuse readers about the authoritative value.
Several criteria are deferred to Tim (needs-tim), creating a single-person bottleneck for quality/aesthetic judgment.
Dense inline errata and cross-references make the file hard to read as a clean single source of truth.
Some criteria mix measured numbers with computed-not-measured values without clearly separating evidence strength.
notes: This is the truth-table gate document for a resident-4B concurrent-cognition build: it defines what 'green' means per subsystem, binds itself to R1-FOLD corrections, and leaves perceptual/quality calls to Tim.
questions: What is the final authoritative swarm concurrency cap after all inline corrections?
Which file or registry entry holds the live max_num_seqs and KV-budget values referenced throughout?
How is a needs-tim criterion promoted to green — is there a sign-off record?
What is the exact relationship between this file and the Implementation Guide + Research Synthesis mentioned in the header?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the common reference truth table for the build; actual content matches that — it enumerates criteria, verification rules, statuses, and dependencies.
apparent_themes: Prove by use, not by assertion.
Determinism and rule-based routing over model confidence values.
Resource realism and measurement over assumed concurrency.
Reflects-never-owns separation between canvas and backend truth.
intention_signals: The build intends to make concurrent multi-role cognition a measured, deterministic, governable substrate that the operator can both observe and author from the canvas, while keeping a resident 4B model within real GPU limits.
novelty: Treats a staged reply as a directed graph of deterministic rules plus concurrent roles with explicit slot-budget algebra, and binds the live visualization to both render and author that graph.
contribution: Provides a verification vocabulary (FUNCTION/FORM, by-use proof, needs-tim) and a structured checklist that a fused system can use to decide when each subsystem is genuinely complete.
summary_for_embedding: A completion-criteria checklist for the Concurrent Cognition build, grouping per-subsystem FUNCTION and FORM gates, verification rules, current status, R1-FOLD corrections, and dependency order.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the binding truth table that determines when the concurrent-cognition substrate and its surfaces are proven by use and ready to fan out.
